response.justification: Wei Chen is a highly experienced individual contributor with a broad technical background spanning 20 years, but the resume contains zero evidence of people management — no direct reports, no hiring, no performance reviews, no team leadership of engineers. The role's core requirement is 2+ years managing people, and this is entirely absent. While there is some tangential developer-tooling work (CI/CD setup at Altom, test framework authorship, MCP tooling at re:cinq), it does not constitute the 3 years of dedicated developer tools engineering the role demands. The candidate's IC trajectory at Staff/Senior SWE levels does not translate to the mid-to-senior management profile sought.
strengths: 20+ years of software development experience across a genuinely diverse stack (embedded, mobile, web, infra), providing strong technical credibility for a productivity-focused engineering org
Demonstrated interest in testing infrastructure and developer experience: authored Fluent-Asserts and Trial (D-lang test runner), set up CI/CD at Altom, and built MCP code generators at re:cinq
Active knowledge-sharing history (workshops, conference talks, Selenium courses) signals the coaching orientation needed for an engineering manager
concerns: No people management experience anywhere in the resume — no mention of direct reports, performance cycles, hiring, or team leadership over other engineers
No evidence of organizational or roadmap ownership; all roles are scoped to individual technical delivery without cross-team alignment or strategic planning responsibilities
Developer tooling work is incidental rather than primary focus — most roles are product or platform engineering, not building compilers, build systems, or testing infrastructure at scale

key_factors.factor: Broad technical depth and teaching history
